name: ST MARGARETS HOSPITAL DURHAM BH3
units: metres below ground level, typed site-investigation log (Allied Exploration & Geotechnics, Forms AEG21/2 cable percussion + AEG22 rotary); depth = base of interval below ground; Reduced Level column cross-checked against the 66.74 m AOD datum

intervals.verbatim: MADE GROUND (Loose, dark grey, fine sandy fill with some fine to coarse gravel and occasional brick fragments.)
Loose, pale brown, fine SAND with some fine to coarse gravel and occasional cobble sized coal fragments.
Loose, brown, fine SAND with many fine gravels.
Loose to medium dense, brown, fine to medium grained, cross bedded SAND with thin, firm, brown, silty clay bands. Below 4.30m BGL ... clay bands becoming more frequent.

Medium dense, brown, slightly silty, fine, micaceous, cross bedded SAND. Between 8.20m and 9.50m BGL ... with thin bands of fine to coarse sand sized coal fragments. Below 10.50m BGL ... becoming slightly clayey.
annotations: continued across Sheets 1 and 2 of 5 ('As sheet 1 of 5')

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense, brown, slightly clayey, fine sandy, thinly cross bedded SILT with thin bands of silty clay.
annotations: with water: general remark records the borehole moist from 14.50m BGL; boring-progress table shows standing water ~20-21.5m from 24.00m depth (10-11.06.92)
continued across Sheets 2 and 3 of 5 ('As sheet 2 of 5')

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense, grey brown, slightly fine sandy, clayey, cross bedded SILT.
Medium dense, brown, silty, micaceous, fine to medium SAND.
Firm to stiff, grey brown, fine sandy, cross bedded, clayey SILT/silty CLAY.
annotations: continued across Sheets 3 and 4 of 5 ('As sheet 3 of 5')

intervals.verbatim: Firm, brown grey, slightly sandy to sandy SILT. Sand predominantly fine.
Brown, sandy GRAVEL and COBBLES with a little clay. Gravel and cobbles predominantly subrounded to rounded. Below 25.50m BGL ... with some soft, brown, very fine sandy silt.

Medium dense to dense, brown, sandy, predominantly rounded to subrounded GRAVEL with some cobbles, occasional boulders and bands of soft to firm, brown, very fine sandy silt. Below 26.60m BGL ... with bands of brown, fine to coarse sand with occasional, predominantly fine, subrounded to rounded gravels.
annotations: methane reading 19% at 26.70m BGL, nil throughout the rest of drilling (general remark, presented as Lower Explosive Limit)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff to very stiff, brown, sandy CLAY with some gravels and occasional cobbles of sandstone, shale, siltstone and mudstone. (Boulder clay)
Firm, grey (in places discoloured orange brown), silty to very silty CLAY, with some fine to medium gravel sized lithorelics of silty mudstone.
annotations: base of drift; 'Cable percussion complete at 33.00m' (Sheet 5 of 5); residual/weathered clay above rockhead, carries silty-mudstone lithorelics

intervals.verbatim: Rotary commenced at 33.00m. Grey, highly to completely weathered, slightly sandy SILTSTONE, very weak.
annotations: top of bedrock (Pennine Coal Measures); logged on the rotary DRILLHOLE LOG, page 7 (Form AEG22)

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey, moderately to highly weathered, sandy SILTSTONE, weak to moderately weak.
Grey, sandy CLAY, with many predominantly fine to medium gravel of mudstone.
annotations: completely weathered mudstone logged as clay with mudstone lithorelics; forms the roof directly above the upper coal

intervals.verbatim: Black, moderately to highly weathered, lustrous COAL, very weak. Thinly laminated with very thin interlaminations of shale.
annotations: upper coal, 0.40m; no seam name given in the original and no pencil seam correlation present

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey, moderately (becoming slightly) weathered, fine sandy SILTSTONE, with occasional very thin interlaminations of coaly shale, moderately weak.
Black, moderately weathered, COAL, very weak, with thin to very thin interlaminations of shale.
annotations: lower coal, 0.45m; no seam name given and no pencil seam correlation present
discontinuity 35.38-35.50m: subvertical fracture, smooth, some light brown discolouration on surface

intervals.verbatim: Black, sandy CLAY, with many gravel of coal.
annotations: thin carbonaceous dirt/parting (0.05m) directly beneath the lower coal, logged as sandy clay with coal gravel; not named seatearth in the original

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey, slightly weathered, slightly fine sandy muddy SILTSTONE, moderately weak. Rotary complete at 36.15m.
annotations: base of hole; 'Rotary complete at 36.15m' — matches index depth
discontinuity 35.60-35.65m: 45 degree joint, with some grey clay infilling
discontinuity 36.07-36.15m: subvertical joint, irregular with a little grey clay infilling

notes: Page 1 = 1967 BGS site-location map (Figure 1, 1:10560), no strata. Page 8 = site plan (Figure, 1:500) with BH03 among BH01-02 and TP1-8, no strata. Pages 2-6 = cable-percussion BOREHOLE RECORD (Form AEG21/2), Sheets 1-5 of 5, drift 0.00-33.00m (made ground over glaciofluvial sand, laminated silt/clay, sand and gravel, boulder clay). Page 7 = rotary DRILLHOLE LOG (Form AEG22, Sheet 1 of 1), hand-annotated 'Duplicate' and stamped NZ24SE/317-a page b; it is the ONLY source for bedrock 33.00-36.15m (Pennine Coal Measures: weathered siltstone with two thin unnamed coals) and does not conflict with the cable-percussion sheets (different depth range). Rotary commenced 33.00m, complete 36.15m = index depth. Every Thickness/Depth/Reduced-Level triple ties to the 66.74 m AOD datum; NO clerk arithmetic slips found. Contractor Allied Exploration & Geotechnics Ltd; client Ove Arup and Partners (Newcastle); logged by G. Giles; contract 1351; drilled 04-13.06.92; status FINAL 15.7.92; date printed 15/09/92. General remarks: description derived from driller's report; borehole moist from 14.50m BGL; methane 19% at 26.70m BGL, nil thereafter (readings as Lower Explosive Limit). No named coal seams and no pencil seam correlations anywhere; the only handwritten marginalia are the BGS reference 'N224SE/317', the grid ref, and BGS registration stamps (ignored per rules). Two thin coals left unnamed: 34.10-34.50m (0.40m) and 35.10-35.55m (0.45m).
